policy: Download only entries with explicit provenance, compatible rights metadata, and approved_for_download set to true.

sources.name: U.S. Copyright Office Classics Protection and Access Act guidance
sources.project_use: Authoritative term reference for pre-1972 sound recording rights analysis.
sources.notes: Use this to evaluate publication-year risk; it is not an audio source.

sources.name: Library of Congress Citizen DJ National Jukebox Folk Music
sources.project_use: First-stop source for public-domain old-time, folk, fiddle, banjo, and early country-adjacent samples.
sources.notes: Use item-level metadata before enabling downloads.

sources.name: Devil's dream reel - excerpt 001

rights_evidence.rights_summary: Citizen DJ states the collection works have been identified as public domain and are free to use and reuse without restriction.
rights_evidence.reuse_scope: Copy, modify, distribute, and perform, including commercial use, without asking permission.
rights_evidence.credit_recommendation: Attribution is recommended but not required; use the Citizen DJ / Library of Congress / National Jukebox credit line.
rights_evidence.cultural_respect_note: LOC asks artists to approach historical recordings with respect and notes privacy or publicity rights may still affect some uses.

sources.credit_line: Citizen DJ Project, Library of Congress, National Jukebox.
sources.project_use: Old-time fiddle reel source for banjo/fiddle chop, granular sampler, and call-and-response arrangement experiments.
sources.notes: Citizen DJ collection page states these works are public domain and may be reused commercially; credit is recommended even when not required.

sources.name: Turkey in the straw medley - excerpt 001

rights_evidence.rights_summary: Citizen DJ states the collection works have been identified as public domain and are free to use and reuse without restriction.
rights_evidence.reuse_scope: Copy, modify, distribute, and perform, including commercial use, without asking permission.
rights_evidence.credit_recommendation: Attribution is recommended but not required; use the Citizen DJ / Library of Congress / National Jukebox credit line.
rights_evidence.cultural_respect_note: LOC warns that historical recordings may reflect language, attitudes, and perspectives of their time; review context before foregrounding this tune.

sources.credit_line: Citizen DJ Project, Library of Congress, National Jukebox.
sources.project_use: Early banjo/traditional-country rhythmic DNA for AeroBand banjo mapping, boom-chuck counter-rhythm, and chopped call-and-response studies.
sources.notes: LOC item metadata describes the 1905 source as a banjo solo with orchestra; use respectfully because some repertoire and title variants have minstrel-era associations.

sources.name: Carnival of Venice - excerpt 001

rights_evidence.rights_summary: Citizen DJ states the collection works have been identified as public domain and are free to use and reuse without restriction.
rights_evidence.reuse_scope: Copy, modify, distribute, and perform, including commercial use, without asking permission.
rights_evidence.credit_recommendation: Attribution is recommended but not required; use the Citizen DJ / Library of Congress / National Jukebox credit line.
rights_evidence.cultural_respect_note: Treat as a historical instrumental source and preserve the collection credit when transformed.

sources.credit_line: Citizen DJ Project, Library of Congress, National Jukebox.
sources.project_use: Virtuosic melodic ornament source for banjo-roll variation, alien granular flourishes, and lead-controller pitch-bend studies.
sources.notes: Selected as old-time/folk-adjacent instrumental technique material from the Citizen DJ public-domain folk sample page.

sources.name: Duke of Marlborough - excerpt 001

rights_evidence.rights_summary: Citizen DJ states the collection works have been identified as public domain and are free to use and reuse without restriction.

rights_evidence.credit_recommendation: Attribution is recommended but not required; use the Citizen DJ / Library of Congress / National Jukebox credit line.
rights_evidence.cultural_respect_note: Treat as historical ballad source material; avoid implying contemporary endorsement by performers or communities.

sources.credit_line: Citizen DJ Project, Library of Congress, National Jukebox.
sources.project_use: Ballad-contour and modal phrase source for A.P. Carter-style narrative hooks, fiddle drone reharmonization, and slow-house vocal-chop beds.
sources.notes: Selected as a public-domain folk sample with useful melodic contour for downtempo arrangement studies.

rights_evidence.cultural_respect_note: Treat as historical instrumental dance material and preserve the collection credit when transformed.

sources.name: Hornpipe - excerpt 001

rights_evidence.rights_summary: Citizen DJ states the collection works have been identified as public domain and are free to use and reuse without restriction.

sources.credit_line: Citizen DJ Project, Library of Congress, National Jukebox.
sources.project_use: Hornpipe contour for loping banjo simulations, return-track dub throws, and spatial fiddle-family counter-lines.

sources.name: Library of Congress Citizen DJ Public Domain Audio Release 2022
sources.project_use: Context for public-domain National Jukebox sound recordings and Citizen DJ reuse.
sources.notes: Use the specific Citizen DJ collection pages for actual candidates.

sources.name: Library of Congress National Jukebox
sources.project_use: Candidate discovery and discographic metadata for early recordings.
sources.notes: Streaming availability does not automatically grant remix/download rights; prefer Citizen DJ public-domain mirrors.

sources.name: Fiddle Tunes of the Old Frontier: The Henry Reed Collection
sources.credit_line: Library of Congress, American Folklife Center
sources.project_use: Old-time fiddle repertoire analysis and melodic vocabulary research.
sources.notes: Excellent stylistic reference; assess item-level rights before sampling.

sources.name: Bluegrass Music: Resources in the American Folklife Center
sources.project_use: Finding aid for bluegrass history, names, and collection discovery.
sources.notes: Research guide only; not a blanket download source.

sources.name: Library of Congress Audio Collections
sources.project_use: Discovery index for old-time, fiddle, banjo, and American folk collections.
sources.notes: Use collection and item rights pages before any reuse.

sources.name: Internet Archive Great 78 Project
sources.project_use: Early string-band, fiddle, banjo, hillbilly, gospel, and country-adjacent recordings.
sources.notes: Archive.org availability is not the same as clearance; verify item metadata and publication date.

sources.name: Internet Archive 78rpm Record Collection
sources.project_use: Deep-cut discovery for pre-1926 string-band and early country records.
sources.notes: Use item pages with Public Domain Mark, CC license, or defensible pre-1926 publication metadata.

sources.name: UCSB Cylinder Audio Archive Early Hillbilly and Old Time Music
sources.project_use: Early cylinder recordings, banjo/fiddle roots, and old-time repertoire.
sources.notes: Prefer pre-1926 items and record item-level rights/publication metadata before enabling downloads. Site-level MP3 metadata includes Creative Commons BY-NC language, so do not approve for distributable production assets without compatible item-level terms.

sources.name: Discography of American Historical Recordings
sources.project_use: Discographic truth source for publication date, label, matrix, performer, and rights checks.
sources.notes: Streaming or metadata access is not blanket reuse permission.

sources.name: Smithsonian Folkways
sources.project_use: Liner notes, historical metadata, and possible licensing leads for old-time and bluegrass repertoire.
sources.notes: Project use requires licensing unless item-specific terms say otherwise.

sources.name: Lomax Digital Archive / Association for Cultural Equity
sources.credit_line: Association for Cultural Equity / Lomax Digital Archive
sources.project_use: Research and rights-holder leads for Appalachian, Southern, and American folk material.
sources.notes: Use as discovery unless permissions are obtained.

sources.name: Freesound CC0/CC-BY search
sources.project_use: Modern one-shots or loops for banjo, fiddle, mandolin, room tone, and percussion when license allows remix/commercial use.
sources.notes: Prefer CC0 or CC-BY; avoid NC/ND for distributable production assets.
